multi_perspective_review.policy: deterministic verdicts (findings + dropped_flags) preserved verbatim; bare abstains triaged into confirmed-correct (advisory note) / advisory / subjective; numbers recomputed before any advisory.

still_abstained_reason.c22: '~5-20%' is a hedged qualitative band over all samples with no bound evidence_refs; not a single recomputable number.
still_abstained_reason.c28: MFR 23/22/25 g/10min are manufacturer datasheet specs (T1 fixed-external) with no reference DB bound in the graph to check against.

routed_to_human.note: reviewed (5-perspective): prose value reconciles with the bound figure/table evidence already in the ClaimGraph and the comparative/superlative wording holds; no over-reach. Dropped the bare abstain. No numeric verifier covers this yet (DESIGN §8 / WS-F synthesis candidate). Basis: PP0 tensile modulus 1957 MPa / yield 39.6 MPa == Fig 15 (1957.2, 39.6); PP0 is the series maximum for BOTH, so 'highest' is faithful.
routed_to_human.quote:
Neat polypropylene (PP0) displayed the highest tensile
modulus (1957 MPa) and yield strength (39.6 MPa)

routed_to_human.note: Abstract states 'cell densities up to 1.1e8 cells/cm3 and reduced average cell sizes of 7.48 um'. Recompute vs Table 3: the true maximum cell density is rPP5 = 1.12e8 (rounded down to 1.1e8 in the abstract), and 7.48 um is the PP5 minimum cell size paired with a density maximum that actually belongs to a different sample (rPP5, D0=7.77). Minor presentation/rounding over-statement, not a data error. Confirm against the unrounded Table 3 values.
routed_to_human.quote:
revealed cell densities up to 1.1 × 108 cells/
cm3 and reduced average cell sizes of 7.48 μm

routed_to_human.note: SEM dispersed-phase particle size 0.38+/-0.02 um (PP/LDPE) and 0.53+/-0.03 um (rPP/LDPE), T4. The number is not recomputable from the ClaimGraph (no per-particle distribution or count). Method appropriateness hinges on sampling N, image magnification, and binarization threshold, none of which are bound. Confirm by checking the Methods for particle-count and sizing protocol.
routed_to_human.quote:
the average
particle size of the dispersed phase was determined to be 0.38
± 0.02 μm in the PP/LDPE samples and 0.53 ± 0.03 μm in
the rPP/LDPE samples

routed_to_human.note: rPP7 dispersed-phase size reported as 0.13+/-0.03 um. In the paper's own field this is implausibly fine: it is ~3-4x smaller than the 0.38-0.53 um measured for the same blend family (c5), and rPP7 (5 phr TMPTA, 40% LDPE, recyclate) would be expected to be at least as coarse, not finer. Likely a typo or single-image outlier. Cannot be settled without the rPP7 SEM micrographs.
routed_to_human.quote:
the average particle size of dispersed
phase was found as 0.13 ± 0.03 μm for rPP7

routed_to_human.note: Gel content rPP7 11% vs PP7 6% (T4). The rPP > PP ordering is chemically plausible (recyclate carries more thermo-oxidative defects / crosslinkable sites), but the absolute gel fractions rest on a solvent-extraction protocol (solvent, temperature, time, hot/cold) that is not bound in the graph. Confirm the extraction method and whether a single replicate or a mean is reported.
routed_to_human.quote:
While rPP7 exhibited 11% gel content, this value was
determined to be 6% for the PP7 sample.

routed_to_human.note: 'theoretical retained value of 20-50% of virgin material market value' is an economics/market claim attributed to prior literature, not a chemistry result of this study, and no citation is bound in the ClaimGraph to check it against. Its correctness is an external-literature + importance judgement, explicitly out of scope for deterministic scoring (DESIGN P5/T8). Route to a human with domain/economics context; would be confirmed by locating and entailment-checking the cited source.
routed_to_human.quote:
with a theoretical
retained value of 20−50% of virgin material market value
